desc:
There is a silence here, profound and forboding, the crouched hush of a predator
at work. The only sound, and the only reminder to travellers that they have not
lost their sense of hearing altogether, is a distant, and echoing drip of water,
which beats like the heart of some ancient, resting beast. The water slips
through the silent, watchful stone, to join deep, dark pools, whose distant
location can only be guessed at. Huge, tapering stalactites mark each entrance
to the cavern, bearing silent witness to the ageless fall of mineral-rich water
in this chamber.
name: A Huge Dripping Cavern

desc:
Where once the echo of many footfalls resounded, now only the sound of dripping
water disturbs the sullen silence. The sloped floor of this road is criss-
crossed by a network of fine cracks, and it is through these that most of the
water escapes, to the unseen depths, below. The water which somehow remains,
follows the westward descent of the roadway.
name: A Wide Road

desc:
The high, domed ceiling of this chamber is a wonder to behold. Millions of tiny
crystals of quartz are embedded in the grey-black granite of the roof, where
they glitter, and twinkle, likes stars in a cloudless sky. Perhaps it was Varda
herself, the greatest and most beautiful among the queens of the Valar, who
wrought this delightful illusion.
name: A Vast Chamber

desc:
A brooding wickedness hangs in the air here, as tangible as the mist which hugs
the cold stone. Water falls harmlessly to the floor, adding to the small, dark
pools, cradled within the rough stone, which occasionally spill over, to send
tiny streams trickling down the passageway.
name: Khoin's Traverse

desc:
Cracks and crevices mark the walls. Bas-reliefs have worn away, the lines of an
artist's chisel filled in by the dust of centuries, the fine detail of dwarven
craft replaced by the artwork of spiderwebs. As the passage twists to the south,
one wall falls away, leaving a gaping precipice to one side of the path, and
from somewhere far below, the sound of water rumbling through the mountain
echoes up toward you.
name: Khoin's Traverse

desc:
Though this roadway is flanked by no wall except to the north, the cold, dark
air somehow seems to close in on all sides. The sense of a chill and evil
presence fills this place, as if this once proud thoroughfare were now plotting
to trick the unwary traveller, who, in that instant of distraction, would plunge
over the edge of the yawning chasm to the south. From somewhere far below, the
distant rumble of water can faintly be heard.
name: Khoin's Traverse

desc:
In the deep, impenetrable darkness, it is hard even to sense where the floor
will be, with each new step. But it rises steadily from east to west, as the
road plunges on, hugging the wall to the north, as if it were afraid of the deep
rift to the south.
name: Khoin's Traverse

desc:
You are in the southwestern corner of a very large cave. At the base of the
greenish walls lie piles of small stones. A draft of cold air, coming from the
east, flows into a hole in the ceiling, at the top of a half-rotten wooden
stair.
name: The Copper Caverns

desc:
You are walking along a twisting, narrow minerary tunnel in the west of
Khazad-dum. To the west the passage continues its tortuous way in the rock of
the Misty Mountains. The tunnel once led to the east as well, but now only
fallen rocks and gravel lie that way. Another exit leads down, on the rotten
steps of a wooden stair, to a large cavern.
name: A Narrow Mine Tunnel

desc:
You have entered a small minerary tunnel, which enlarges to the north and
continues its narrow course to the east. Here and there you can see the
abandoned tools of the Dwarven miners, most of them completely ruined by the
ravages of time. A faint air current flows in from the north.
name: A Narrow Mine Tunnel

desc:
You are on a curving rampway which ascends to the west and descends to the
south. The walls are rough-cut, and the surface is smooth but has had shallow
grooves and undulations worn in it by the boots of generations of Dwarven
miners.
name: Rampway into a Mine

desc:
You are at the south end of a very long chamber which seems once to have
provided bed space to the dwarves who worked in the mine. Niches on either side
of a north-south corridor, arranged three high, seem to have provided bed space.
These wall-bed spaces are actually neatly carved and may once have provided
comfortable accommodations, from a dwarf's perspective at any rate. Every usable
scrap of cloth, wood, or metal has been looted long ago.
name: Miners' Barracks

desc:
You are in a network of rooms which were once part of the operations of the
Dwarven mine. Some seem to be supply rooms, others the personal quarters of
important Dwarves. Most of the doors are either locked or blocked by rubble.
However, a tunnel has been hacked through the area, piercing the walls of the
rooms by brute force. From here it proceeds west and burrows into the natural
stone; northward, it continues toward an unknown destination.
name: A Tunnel through Walls

desc:
You are at the north end of a very long room which was once the barracks of the
Dwarven miners. At this end are the baths, latrines, and cooking facilities. The
beds are carved into the long walls of the chamber which retreat southward into
the darkness. There seems to be a large pile of rubble here in the latrine area.
name: Miners' Barracks

desc:
This appears to be a room of importance. Its walls are polished, and the
junction between the walls and the ceiling is adorned by an ornamental frieze.
There is wooden furniture here, including a long counter with a polished crystal
top. Some paintings adorn the east wall. An arch with a design on the keystone
leads west; to its left is what looks like a lock with four keyholes, mounted in
the wall and with no connection to any obvious door. To the north a heavy grille
blocks the way to the north, and a heavy steel door blocks the doorway to the
south.
name: Mine Office

desc:
This is a short corridor, whose walls are unadorned except for some vertical
cracks or panels that run from floor to ceiling. The skeleton of an orc lies on
the floor here; the bones seem to have been crushed flat in some way. There are
some other marks, like heavy cart tracks, that seem to run across the corridor,
from one wall to the other.
name: Vestibule

desc:
This small room is lined by drawers which extend into the walls. Many of the
drawers have been pulled out and left open. The construction of the drawers is
very precise and sturdy indeed, and they still travel freely in their tracks
centuries since they were last used. On the west side of the room, however, the
drawers have been displaced from where a tunnel has been dug through the solid
stone.
name: Mithril Storage

desc:
This tunnel is very low, about three feet in height, with irregular walls and
ceiling, and still contains much of the rubble that was created during its
digging. Still, it seems to get some use. The gravel and stones on its floor are
packed down, and there is sort of an animal smell.
name: Rough Tunnel

desc:
This was once the working face of a mine tunnel. It is partially hidden by
shoring installed by the dwarves, and it must have been abandoned long before
they dug too deep and uncovered the fell creature that chased them from their
homes. The tunnel extends back to the west; some of the shoring has been
removed, and a smaller tunnel has been dug to the east.
name: End of a Mine Tunnel

desc:
This east-west mine tunnel has apparently suffered a partial collapse. Slabs
have fallen from the ceiling and lie in heaps on both the north and south walls.
A crevice stretches upward, inaccessible. The passage continues east and west.
name: Partially Caved-in Tunnel

desc:
This is a broad, plain mine tunnel with a pronounced downward slope to the west.
The tunnel floor has little dust on it, but there are some odd bits of rubbish -
bones, teeth, discarded useless armour...
name: A Sloping Tunnel

desc:
The wet stone floor is very slippery at this point. As always in Moria, there
are no handrails, so the stairs which begin here could be quite dangerous to
walk down, especially in this darkness. The tunnel bends from the east to the
north, descending all the time.
name: Spiralling Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel slopes upward to the west here. The air currents waver and change
direction here. Now a cooler breeze will blow down from above, now a warmer,
more humid, and more pungent air will waft up from below and eastward.
name: Sloping Windy Tunnel

desc:
This long stretch of tunnel has a very steep grade as it slowly curves to the
west and south from this point. There is a slight current in the air here, a
small draft rising from the southern area into the upper tunnels to the west,
which carries the strong scent of death and decay from below. The walls and rock
floor here show a few traces of the movement of orc patrols through this area,
ranging from a broken speartip wedged into a wall crack to a pile of orc spoor
directly underfoot.
name: Bend in the Sloping Tunnel

desc:
A steep north-south tunnel intersects with a narrow tunnel running to the east,
forming this small junction chamber. The walls and roof here are split with wide
cracks and large gaps in the ancient stonework, some of which have been patched
in haphazard fashion with black mud and small stones. Orc scrawlings and symbols
cover all surfaces here, some old and scratched out and others fresh and full of
new malice.
name: Junction

desc:
This precarious passage plunges downward as it travels due south, and it
eventually ends in a mass of rubble which completely blocks any further progress
in that direction. The roof has given way here and may do so again at any time,
and this prospect forces a retreat into the safer areas of the tunnel to the
north.
name: Deadend Tunnel

desc:
This narrow passage steadily descends as it travels from the east to the west.
The floor is coated with a thick layer of decaying filth which results in very
uncertain footing and a strong stench everywhere nearby. All walls are covered
with bloody splotches, long scratches and depictions of the lidless Red Eye.
name: Narrow Passage

desc:
The cramped passageway turns sharply west and north from this point, a small
straight section of tunnel running past a massive irondoor set in the south
wall. The passage rises and becomes wider to the west, and plunges down after it
bends north where it appears to taper off to little more than a wide fissure in
the rock. Most of the passage walls and floor are covered by filth, scratches
and orc scrawlings, but the east wall has managed to avoid this fate.
name: Bend in the Narrow Passage

desc:
The walls of this tunnel close in tightly here, limiting passage to small groups
of thin individuals travelling in single-file order. Much of the surrounding
rock is quite sharp, and there are small drabs of dried blood on shard edges and
spattered in spots on the ground. This tunnel appears to widen towards the
south, but narrows even further to the north.
name: Very Narrow Passage

desc:
There is not much to see in this very small and tight space. One creature can
fit inside this crevice at any given time, and any attempt to squeeze another
inside would result in pools of blood and screams. There are tight exits to the
east and south, but care is required to move in any direction to avoid slicing
flesh off on the hundreds of sharp rock shards here.
name: Narrow Crevice

desc:
The southern terminus of the rough, wide tunnel appears to be here at this small
cul-de-sac formed by three highly polished black stone walls. None of the
surfaces here has any mark or scratch, nor is there any debris littering the
passageway which would indicate any recent use of this place. The tunnel climbs
sharply north from this deadend, and it is wide enough to permit a few creatures
to pass side-by-side as they travel upwards.
name: End of the Hidden Tunnel

desc:
The passage has become very wide here, wide enough for a small group to walk
comfortably abreast either north or south. The grade of the passage is very
steep for an underground passageway, and there are no signs that anyone has
passed this point in a long, long time.
name: Hidden Tunnel

desc:
The rough tunnel continues north and south here, passing through dark black
veins of stone. The tunnel walls and roof appear rough-cut, showing little of
the skill and care usually given by Dwarves to their stonecraft nor any sign
that they were formed by some natural process of erosion. The tunnel slopes
sharply downward as it plunges into utter blackness to the south.
name: Hidden Tunnel

desc:
The passage comes to a dead-end rather abruptly in a heavily scored rock wall.
There are no apparent exits, except the one through which you came. Though
clearly being a mined passage, and not one formed by the forces of nature, it is
obvious that those who once built this tunnel had no intention of making it a
good job, leaving rough rocky walls and ground as they were as soon as the job
of tunnelling through the hard rock had been finished. A rank smell fills the
air and dampness clings to your clothes.
name: A Dead-end Passage

desc:
You are standing at the foot of a narrow, twisting stairway. It winds away up
and into the dank fastness of the mountain rock. This small chamber appears
unfinished and fairly crudely hewn - almost as if it was halted prematurely.
Moisture hangs in the air giving a very damp feel to the place, though no
puddles have formed. Seemingly, the dwarves who built these stairs aborted their
work before it was finished, perhaps called away for other, more important
tasks. This is also evidenced by the rough walls of the cavern; they have not
yet been entirely smoothed and worked to a professional finish, like most other
Dwarven tunnels.
name: The Foot of Some Winding Stairs

desc:
You are in a cramped and twisting tunnel which runs generally north and south.
Its walls, floor, and ceiling are composed of loose rocks; the ceiling has been
haphazardly shored with stone slabs and wooden posts. It is about four feet high
at best, and repeatedly zigzags up, left, down and right, and in every
combination of those. Here and there it bores through a more solid wall or takes
advantage of a pre-existing doorway. A sharp, animal reek is everywhere.
name: Burrow through Rocks

desc:
You are in a room which was formerly an old mine tunnel or gallery, whose long
dimension runs east and west. Long ago it was apparently abandoned and filled
with loose stone. Much of the stone was later removed again, but the floor is
still very irregular and composed of large and small rocks packed together. The
room is pervaded by the smells of urine, dung, and rotting flesh.
name: Rocky Mine Tunnel

desc:
You are in part of an old mine tunnel which was later re-filled with loose
stone. Some of the stone has been removed here to make a bowl-shaped space,
whose floor and sides are made of compacted rocks and whose ceiling is the roof
of the Dwarven tunnel. The stones of the floor, however, are buried in layers of
bones, dung, and animal hair. The pungent reek of animal urine is overpowering.
name: Wolves' Den

desc:
You are in what may be an old mine tunnel junction, but the area is so piled
with clutter that it is hard to get a clear sense of where the walls are. Ore
carts and skids, bins, metal beams, stone slabs, and the like have been thrown
upon each other in a terrible hodge-podge. A sort of burrow snakes through the
pile from east to west.
name: Cluttered Tunnel

desc:
This is a steep tunnel which spirals counterclockwise and downward. By sliding
along the walls it is possible to keep your balance here. The smudges on the
outside wall suggest that others have done the same before you.
name: A Sloping Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel's steepness is accentuated by a layer of slick slimy mud which has
been deposited by a trickle of foul-smelling water which leaks from the tunnel's
wall. To remedy the situation, it seems that someone has hacked crude steps or
footholds in the stone of the tunnel floor.
name: Winding Spiralling Tunnel

desc:
This is the eastern end of a mine gallery which here intersects a north-south
access tunnel. The stone floor, however, is covered with foul hard soil, and
furthermore is heavily littered with bones and trash. Overhead is a black,
square, inaccessible hole; in the floor is a similar hole covered with an
immovable iron grating. Over this grating a makeshift stone hearth has been
constructed. Red embers glow dully, and their light does nothing to brighten the
room.
name: End of a Gallery

desc:
This is a stretch of tunnel about forty feet long which is blocked by a crude
wall of loose stone and wood scraps to the south. At the north end is a
stairwell with flights of steps going up and down. A heavy wooden bin, which
seems as old as the room itself, contains some scimitars, daggers, and shields.
name: Weapons Room

desc:
Much of the floor of the access tunnel here is piled with large stones which
clatter loudly against each other as you tread on them. There is little here of
interest. The air is stuffy, damp, and thick with a pervasive foul smell.
name: An Access Tunnel

desc:
This chamber, which is really just a featureless dead end in a stone tunnel, is
evidently used as a sleeping area. Large, filthy pelts are strewn about, and
there are some wads of bloody and matted cloth which may once have been
garments. You see no luxuries or even furniture.
name: A Sleeping Chamber

desc:
The smell of offal and excrement is overpowering here. This room may once have
been used for storage of tools or for the administration of the mine, but now it
is a combination garbage dump and latrine. A hole in the floor, about a foot
across, leads down into the depths; this is where the waste is supposed to go,
it seems, but not all of it has gone there yet.
name: The Garbage Pit

desc:
You are at a landing on a stairway which descends as a square-cornered helix
around a central pillar. The steps going up from here are blocked by a huge
chunk of stone which has fallen in from the walls. Other cracks mark the walls
of the stairwell here. A low, narrow corridor with an arched cross section goes
south from the landing.
name: A Landing

desc:
A low and narrow passage with an arched roof makes a right-angled turn here. On
the west wall of the angle, a niche has been crudely cut where some people could
stand unobserved from the passage to the north. In addition, an ore cart has
been propped on its end as shelter for the niche. The passage continues to the
east.
name: Bend in a Narrow Passage

desc:
This room may once have had some something to do with the processing of ore;
that would account for the long stone troughs and the water that trickles down
from a pipe. Now, however, the most prominent feature is the rotting severed
heads, stuck on stakes, which decorate this end of the room. The orkish
commander who has taken this for his quarters finds nothing so pleasant as the
heads of his enemies of other orc tribes or of his own. A guard post has also
been established here in the north end of the room.
name: Dimly Lit Room

desc:
The south end of this long room has been taken over by the commander of this
Durghash outpost for his personal use. Here are his stew pots, his pelts, and
the skins and teeth of his enemies. Here is a cooking fire using real wood from
the outside. Here is his private food supply, well stocked when every other orc
is hungry.
name: Dimly Lit Room

desc:
You are on a stone staircase which ascends by means of short flights, north,
east, south, and west, around a cylindrical central pillar. The work is not
elegant, but precise. Below you, however, the stairway has been filled with
loose boulders up to eighteen inches across. There is no way to descend the
stairs. However, by the landing here is a slanting door which may conceal a
downward-leading path.
name: Blocked Stairwell

desc:
This was once the top of a metal-walled chute, but it was blocked long ago,
either by a rockfall or by deliberately jamming it with stone. The upper four
feet of the chute are now a cramped, stinking cell. The irregular boulders
forming its floor are caked with body waste. In a cavity between two rocks you
see a corpse, and some other bones are scattered around.
name: Tiny Cell

desc:
Squeezing your way in to this very narrow passage, the fact is made obvious that
it was not made for everyday travel. Lots of dust has accumulated here, and the
sharp, rocky walls almost seem to get closer to you each second. The passage
seems to continue to the east but you cannot make out if it gets even more
narrow or if it widens up. The north wall seems to be a door of some sort.
name: Narrow Accessway

desc:
This is a small compartment, empty except for some peculiar metal objects
partially embedded in the north wall. These include a lever which sticks out of
the wall. The craftsmanship is clearly Dwarven, but the mechanism seems to have
been recently greased.
name: Below the Grate

desc:
This access passage could not have been spacious even for dwarves; for larger
beings it is a tight squeeze indeed. There is a wider space in the middle,
though, which someone seems to occupy much of the time. At any rate, there is a
water pot, some discarded bones, and a distinct smell of urine.
name: Narrow Accessway

desc:
The tunnel bends sharply downward as it curves broadly from the west to the
north. Its floor and the lower three feet of its side walls seem to be made of
black marble and are polished to a high and slippery gloss. There are a pair of
eyebolts screwed in the wall on either side. A side passage from the east joins
the main tunnel in its downward plunge. You hear a distinct rumbling sound
coming up the tunnel from the north.
name: Smooth Descending Road

desc:
Here, where an important road surfaced in black stone originally turned from the
south to the west, there has been a massive slippage of the mountain's rock. The
westward course of the road seems to stop short at a solid cliff. The whole
northwest face of the Silvertine must have settled downward twenty feet to
create this thorough a blockage. Perhaps it was undermined by careless
excavations. A branch of the dark road turns eastward and downward here. There
is some writing on the inside wall of this bend.
name: Dark Road

desc:
You are about a foot away from a fatal plunge into a black and horrifying abyss.
The treacherous road plunges sharply down and becomes a sheer drop-off. Below
you is a roaring cauldron of water; the spume leaps up and licks hungrily at
your face and hands. You are holding yourself in place by using a minuscule
toehold in the angle between the floor and the wall of the tunnel. Unless you
are as confident a stoneclimber as the original builders of this place, the
trembling rock will probably shake you loose quite soon...
name: At the Brink

desc:
Your eyes are now getting used to the amazing darkness of this tunnel, and
bending down you notice that the slick stone paving is wet and moist. Also, it
seems to be covered by some kind of fungus, although it has worn smooth in
places - perhaps this tunnel is used still today. The tunnel spirals downward
from the south to the east.
name: Spiralling Tunnel

desc:
You lose your footing and hurtle downwards into thundering blackness. You bounce
off a loose rocky surface with an agonising crash, and hurtle out into a
spume-filled void.. Beneath the spume, torrential currents of unimaginable force
swirl and collide endlessly, moving stones as if they were dry leaves and
grinding them to powder against each other. Over the centuries, thousands of
tons of rubble and slag - the discarded bones of Celebdil - were dumped here by
the dwarves, to be reduced to slurry by the dark underground waters and carried
away to an unimaginable destination. The same now happens to you.
name: A Churning Maelstrom of Water
